items.title: A 'ring of fire' could soon burn around Melbourne

children.content: Australian states are pushing ahead with waste-to-energy incineration, despite significant opposition. It means Melbourne will soon be surrounded by a 'ring of fire'.

avatar.alt: Norman Hermant is the ABC’s Moscow correspondent.

items.title: Three more teens charged over alleged stabbing of homeless man

children.content: Police charge a further three teens following the alleged stabbing of a homeless man in the regional Victorian city of Bendigo last week.

image.altText: outside of pub in bendigo

items.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan decries sexist Melbourne billboard

children.content: Premier Jacinta Allan has been joined by Anthony Albanese, Julia Gillard and other politicians — not including Pauline Hanson — in denouncing the language used to describe her on political signs seen in the Melbourne CBD.

image.altText: A billboard saying Ditch the Witch

items.title: Victorian teachers sue over alleged unpaid superannuation

children.content: A class action alleges the Education Department short-changed teachers some $100 million in superannuation contributions between 1995 and 2023.

image.altText: A woman with chin length wavy blonde hair with a two-toned orange scarf around her neck sits with patchworking on her lap.

items.title: Town saved from flooding 70 years ago sets vintage tractor record

children.content: A New South Wales town hosts a celebration of the "little grey" tractors that helped save it from flooding 70 years ago.

image.altText: A man walks towards a grey vintage tractor with a red canopy

items.title: Celebrities slip and slide in first Big Freeze event since Neale Daniher's death

children.content: The MCG has hosted the first Big Freeze MND fundraiser since the death of AFL legend and motor neurone disease campaigner, Neale Daniher.

gallery.altText: Weather presenter Sam Mac participates in the Big Freeze event dressed as children's character Bluey
A large light pole stands over a packed sports stadium grandstand where nearly all the crowd is wearing a blue two-toned beanie.
Former AFL player Nathan Jones participates in the Big Freeze - a slippery slide into an ice bath

thumbnail.altText: Weather presenter Sam Mac participates in the Big Freeze event dressed as children's character Bluey
A large light pole stands over a packed sports stadium grandstand where nearly all the crowd is wearing a blue two-toned beanie.
Former AFL player Nathan Jones participates in the Big Freeze - a slippery slide into an ice bath

items.title: Three adults, four children flee suspicious Melbourne house fire

children.content: Police say seven people are lucky to have escaped a suspected arson attack in one of three house fires across Melbourne's north overnight.

image.altText: Suburban brick hourse with broken windows and charcoaled guttering and roof

items.title: Does being in a 'safe seat' influence whether your hospital gets an upgrade?

children.content: Are hospitals in safe seats being overlooked for multi-million-dollar refurbishments by the Victorian government?

image.altText: Warrnambool base hospital

items.title: Missing Melbourne teacher allegedly murdered by brother in India

children.content: Indian authorities allege Melbourne maths teacher Sunil Sharma was murdered by his brother and dumped in a canal. He had been in India to prepare a property for sale.

image.altText: Four people with face covering stand stand by side book-ended by two men in turbans and khaki uniforms.

gallery.altText: Relatives of missing Melbourne man Sunil Sharma who are accused in relation to his disappearance and death

thumbnail.altText: Relatives of missing Melbourne man Sunil Sharma who are accused in relation to his disappearance and death

items.title: Commuters call for action after machete brawl at Flinders Street Station

children.content: Police say up to 10 people were fighting inside the station on Saturday night before some boarded a train still armed with machetes.

image.altText: A number of police officers stand on a platform beside a train with a person sitting on the ground near an officer.

items.title: Boy on bail charged over stabbing of homeless man in Bendigo

children.content: Police have charged a 16-year-old boy who was on bail over the stabbing of a homeless man in Bendigo on Thursday night.

image.altText: outside of Sporting Globe building

items.title: Take a look inside 170-year-old timber church in Victoria

children.content: The drop slab design of Victoria's oldest timber church is a testament to 19th-century engineering.

image.altText: exterior of Christ Church Tarravilla

items.title: Hawks forward target of racist online abuse after loss to Bulldogs

children.content: The AFL and Hawthorn come out in support of Mabior Chol after the Hawks forward shares a series of abusive messages he received after Friday night's match against the Bulldogs.

image.altText: Mabior Chol takes a mark for the Hawks during an AFL match.

items.title: When a detective told a witness Joanna was pregnant, she took police to VCAT

children.content: A woman who says she suffered life-changing stress and anxiety — and later, the death of one of her babies — after a Victoria Police officer unlawfully disclosed that she was pregnant wants her story known.

gallery.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

thumbnail.altText: A woman with brunette hair poses for a photo in a leafy setting wearing a pink top and blue scarf

items.title: Why Victoria's upper house voting regime leaves much to be desired

children.content: Victoria's democratic system is a bit dodgy. That's not a view from a keyboard warrior, but one shared by many across the political spectrum.

image.altText: A Victorian Electoral Commission white-coloured ballot box

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: The fake police scam stealing millions from Australia's Chinese community

children.content: Justin thought he was being investigated by the Chinese police, but was pulled into a vicious and frightening scam targeting the Chinese diaspora all over the world.

image.altText: A man looks worried as he speaks to someone over the phone.

items.title: Tap-and-go payments introduced to myki technology on Victorian train network

children.content: Melbourne commuters will be able to use their credit card, phone or smartwatch across the train network this month, joining other major cities with ticketless travel after repeated delays.

image.altText: A hand holds up a mobile phone which is displaying a credit card to a yellow payment screen on a bus that has a tick on it.

items.title: What's driving Australia's lentil boom?

children.content: Australia's winter crop production is expected to be down this year, but lentils are bucking the trend and headed towards a record.

image.altText: Three glass jars. One filled with red lentils, one with yellow lentils and the third with barley.

items.title: At Melbourne's last video rental store, physical media never died

children.content: CDs, DVDs, Blu-Rays and even VHS cassettes are enjoying a resurgence. Why some are returning to physical media?

gallery.altText: Man in black clap and black tee shirt standing at the door of a video store

thumbnail.altText: Man in black clap and black tee shirt standing at the door of a video store

avatar.alt: A man smiles as he presents the television news.

items.title: Truck left hanging from bridge after freeway rollover in Melbourne's west

children.content: A truck has spilled its entire load after rolling at a major junction of the Princes Freeway, M80 Ring Road and West Gate Bridge. Emergency services have extricated the truck driver and rushed her to hospital.

image.altText: White sheeting is strewn down a grassy hill underneath a concrete bridge where a damaged truck is parked.

items.title: The 20-minute coffee van that keeps a small community together

children.content: Residents of the tiny town of Underbool, in Victoria's remote Mallee region, flock to this small coffee van, which has even smaller opening hours.

image.altText: Elderly gentleman in plaid jacket and hat with hand on coffee van counter, a woman and a girl working in coffee van

items.title: Mental health royal commission update silent on hundreds of promised homes

children.content: A progress report on a major mental health inquiry sparks fears that a major housing commitment has fallen to the wayside.

items.title: Man charged following siege in Melbourne's inner west

children.content: Neighbours waited anxiously on Friday morning until the siege was resolved. Victoria Police said it took about one and a half hours for the man to surrender.

image.altText: Police officer wearing tactical gear at the top of an armoured vehicle.

items.title: Delays, red tape frustrate parents begging for school crossing

children.content: Mel Sharples began pushing for a pedestrian crossing at Loch Primary School when her eldest son, Marley, was in prep. Seven years later, she's still fighting.

image.altText: Image of a group of women with arms crossed, standing outside along a main street.

items.title: Teen who allegedly stormed plane with shotgun to face adult court

children.content: A Victorian teenager who allegedly tried to hijack a plane has failed in a bid to keep his case in the Children's Court, where the maximum penalty is four years in jail.

image.altText: Several people holding down a teenage boy in an aeroplane

items.title: 'ISIS bride' charged with slavery poses risk if granted bail, court hears

children.content: Zeinab Ahmad, who recently returned to Australia, is seeking bail after she was charged with slavery offences after allegedly crossing into Syria with her family in 2015.

image.altText: A court sketch of Zeinab Ahmad

items.title: Victoria's top cop on when the scourge of firebombings will stop

children.content: Criminals in the construction sector, youth offending and station staffing are discussed in a wide-ranging interview with Victoria Police Chief Commissioner Mike Bush.

articles.title: Properties searched during investigation into Melbourne arson attacks

children.content: Police seize a tomahawk, cash, steroids, illicit tobacco, mobile phones and other devices while searching seven properties this morning, following ongoing attacks at Melbourne hospitality venues.

image.altText: Fire engines parked in a street at night

gallery.altText: Victoria Police Chief Commissioner Mike Bush

thumbnail.altText: Victoria Police Chief Commissioner Mike Bush

items.title: No place like home with rescuers for Dorothy the kitten who survived the unthinkable

children.content: A kitten left for dead after being thrown from a moving car has spent months recovering and is now at a new home with two familiar faces.

image.altText: A grey kitten with yellow eyes, lying ina  bed, with a bandage around one of its legs.

items.title: Former judge facing new child sex charges

children.content: 75-year-old Alan Boulton is accused of travelling outside Australia and engaging in sexual activity with a child under 16 a decade ago, according to charge sheets.

image.altText: A photo of the Monash University law chambers in Melbourne's CBD.

items.title: CCTV vision shows teens' brutal attack on homeless man

children.content: Police say the man stumbled into a sports bar with stab wounds and collapsed.

image.altText: CCTV of a street at night time, group of four walking down the road.

items.title: Victoria 'failing the whole nation' in enforcement of anti-vaping measures

children.content: Anti-vaping advocates have launched a new campaign warning young people of the health dangers posed by vaping. They say laws in Victoria are not helping their cause.

image.altText: A hand holding a purple vape, Bunbury tower in the background
